data.title: Wasion Plans HK$1.5 Billion Top-Up Share Placing and Subscription
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Wasion Holdings Limited ( (HK:3393) ) has shared an announcement.

Wasion Holdings Limited has entered into a placing and subscription agreement involving a top-up placing of up to 50,000,000 existing shares and a matching subscription of up to 50,000,000 new shares at HK$30.00 per share. Each tranche represents about 5.02% of the current issued share capital and approximately 4.78% of the enlarged share capital, with the new shares to be issued under the company’s general mandate.

The transaction, coordinated by placing agents on a best-efforts basis, is structured so that the vendor sells existing shares to investors and then subscribes for an equivalent number of new shares, effectively replenishing the company’s capital base. The deal remains conditional on various requirements, including approval for listing of the new shares on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, and the company has cautioned shareholders and potential investors that the placing and subscription may or may not proceed.

More about Wasion Holdings Limited

Wasion Holdings Limited, incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong, operates in the broader technology and industrial sector, focusing on products and services that involve equity financing through the capital markets. The company uses share placements and subscriptions under general mandates as part of its treasury and capital management strategy to support ongoing corporate development and maintain market presence.

data.title: Silkwave Signs MOU with HKS to Build Satellite-Driven Converged Media Services
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

CMMB Vision Holdings ( (HK:0471) ) has provided an update.

Silkwave Inc has signed a non-binding memorandum of understanding with Hong Kong TV International Media Group Ltd to combine its satellite transmission capabilities with HKS’s global premium media content. The collaboration aims to build satellite-connected multimedia services for vehicles, support “satellite + 5G” content offloading, and provide emergency broadcasting and public service channels.

The partners plan to jointly expand converged media platforms in overseas and ASEAN markets by leveraging Silkwave’s Belt and Road satellite network and HKS’s multi-satellite TV footprint, which covers more than 150 countries. While the agreement is only an expression of intent at this stage, the board expects the initiative to strengthen Silkwave’s regional media presence and benefit shareholders if definitive contracts are concluded.

More about CMMB Vision Holdings

Silkwave Inc is a Cayman Islands–incorporated company listed in Hong Kong that focuses on satellite communication transmission technology and connected multimedia services. The group operates a satellite data network aligned with the Belt and Road initiative and targets applications such as in-vehicle connectivity and converged media distribution across Asia and other international markets.

data.title: 160 Health Expands “160AI Hospital” Network to 102 Public Institutions in China
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

160 Health International Ltd. ( (HK:2656) ) has shared an update.

160 Health International Limited reported that from January 2025 to March 31, 2026, its “160AI Hospital” collaboration model expanded to 102 public medical institutions, up from 82 at the end of 2025. Of these, 54 are Class IIIA and 78 are Class III or above, underscoring strong traction in China’s high-end hospital segment and broad recognition of the Group’s digital healthcare capabilities.

The partner hospitals collectively handle about 84.37 million outpatient visits annually and generate around RMB99.7 billion in yearly revenue, highlighting the substantial operational scale underpinning the company’s platform. Management said the model has moved from pilot to large-scale rollout, and the Group is accelerating deployment of its “Medical Digital Staff” AI systems to deepen integration in clinical workflows, further extend its national network, and support high-quality growth of its digital healthcare business, while cautioning that overall performance remains subject to market and policy uncertainties.

More about 160 Health International Ltd.

160 Health International Limited is a Hong Kong–listed company operating in the digital healthcare and wellness solutions sector. Its core offerings include the “160AI Hospital” operational collaboration model and related AI-enabled systems, aimed at supporting the digital transformation and intelligent upgrading of public medical institutions, particularly higher-tier Class III hospitals across China.

data.title: Manycore Tech Fully Exercises Over-Allotment Option After Strong IPO Demand
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Manycore Tech Inc. ( (HK:0068) ) has provided an update.

Manycore Tech Inc. has announced the full exercise of the over-allotment option in its global share offering, resulting in the issuance of 24,092,500 additional shares, or about 15% of the initial offer size, at HK$7.62 per share. The new shares, which will commence trading on April 23, 2026, will be used to return stock borrowed from Wintermatch International Limited, bringing the company’s total issued share capital to 1,726,199,340 shares and slightly increasing the public float, signaling strong demand for the IPO and reinforcing market confidence in the stock.

Following the completion of the over-allotment, shares issued under the global offering will account for 10.70% of the company’s enlarged share capital, compared with 9.45% previously. The adjustment reflects a modest dilution of existing holdings but supports liquidity and trading stability in the post-listing period, while another announcement is expected after the end of the stabilization period under Hong Kong securities rules.

More about Manycore Tech Inc.

Manycore Tech Inc., incorporated in the Cayman Islands, is a technology company listed on the Main Board of the Hong Kong Stock Exchange under stock code 00068. The company recently conducted a global offering of its shares, targeting international investors through both Hong Kong and overseas tranches to broaden its shareholder base and enhance its capital structure.

Xiaocaiyuan International Holding Ltd. has declared a final ordinary cash dividend of RMB 0.2125 per share for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, with shareholder approval granted on 20 April 2026. The dividend for shares registered in Hong Kong will be paid in Hong Kong dollars at HKD 0.2425 per share, based on an exchange rate of RMB 1 to HKD 1.141266, with an ex-dividend date of 22 April 2026.

The record date for determining shareholder entitlement is set for 27 April 2026, following a book closure period from 24 to 27 April, and the dividend payment is scheduled for 11 May 2026 via Tricor Investor Services Limited as share registrar. The announcement, which updates prior information on the default currency and exchange rate, provides clarity for investors on the timing and local-currency amount of distributions, underlining the company’s ongoing return of cash to shareholders.

data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

AustAsia Group Limited. ( (HK:2425) ) just unveiled an update.

AustAsia Group has announced a planned leadership transition, with long-serving chief executive and executive director Edgar Dowse Collins resigning effective 20 April 2026 as part of the company’s succession plan. Current chief operating officer and executive director Yang Ku will take over as CEO from 21 April 2026, while operation general manager Zhang Haicheng will be promoted to COO, moves the board says are designed to ensure continuity and an orderly management handover.

Yang, who has over three decades of experience in the dairy sector and has been with the group since 2009, will receive an annual remuneration of RMB3.96 million plus a performance-linked bonus under the company’s remuneration policies. The company emphasized that Collins is leaving without disagreement with the board, signaling a stable, internally driven transition that is expected to support operational consistency and ongoing execution of AustAsia’s strategy.

More about AustAsia Group Limited.

AustAsia Group Ltd., incorporated in Singapore and listed in Hong Kong, operates in the dairy industry through a network of subsidiaries. The group focuses on large-scale dairy farming and related operations in China, supported by executives with deep technical and production management experience in animal husbandry and feed science.

Chery Automobile Co.,Ltd. ( (HK:9973) ) has provided an announcement.

Chery Automobile has convened its annual general meeting for 20 May 2026 in Wuhu, where shareholders will review the board and supervisory committee work reports, the 2025 annual and ESG reports, and a proposal for providing guarantees to subsidiaries and related companies. They will also vote on a final dividend of RMB0.86 per share for 2025, re-appoint Ernst & Young as auditor, and elect a new slate of board directors and supervisory committee members, decisions that will shape the company’s governance, capital allocation and risk management in the coming year.

More about Chery Automobile Co.,Ltd.

Chery Automobile Co., Ltd. is a Chinese automaker based in Wuhu, Anhui Province, operating as a joint stock company with limited liability. Listed in Hong Kong under stock code 9973, it focuses on the design, manufacturing and sale of automobiles and related technologies, serving both domestic and international markets.
